displayTitle: Accept or Reject: The Code Makes the Call

beats.narration: Finding an indication is technique. Dispositioning it is law: accept or reject comes from the governing code and your written procedure, never your opinion.

payload.title: DISPOSITION IS LAW
payload.sub: Accept or reject comes from the rulebook, not the technician.

beats.narration: Two philosophies. Workmanship judges process control: amplitude versus reference, plus length. Fitness-for-service asks whether the flaw will fail, demanding accurate sizing.
Pressure codes build a DAC from side-drilled holes. Cracks, lack of fusion, incomplete penetration reject at any amplitude; others above reference reject on length.

payload.heading: Dispositioned against the DAC
payload.note: PLANAR: ANY AMPLITUDE · OTHERS: REF + LENGTH

beats.narration: Structural codes run pure decibel arithmetic: indication level, minus reference, minus attenuation gives a rating the tables turn into accept, reject, or repair.
Same physics, different bookkeeping: the systems never interchange. Measure honestly, compare to the written rule, and let the code make the call.

payload.sub: Same physics — never blend the rulebooks.
